author: @justme.9711
text: There are many generator options that are cheap and very practical and very effective, BUT! the problem is storage. What is desperately needed is a public access plan to make a battery that can run a house. Lead acid is ancient, but IT WORKS. A 200lt/44gallon plastic drum or IBC containers made into DC batteries and a row/battery of these and we have a viable storage option. The tech is over 100 years old. A 12v or 24v group of batteries of that size would be life changing all around the world and the materials are all very common. Drums or IBC 1000 lt containers put out of the sun and filled with hydrocloric acid [ cheap ]. PLEASE somebody focus on this and crack it.

author: @ChristieNel
text: That's half the equation.  Load matching is the other half.  Open circuit voltage is meaningless - you need a load.  And to get the right load to get peak power, you should use your MPPT and measure Watt true RMS. Or experiment with load resistors to find peak Watt.

replies.author: @ChristieNel
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering But why? A fast turbine is not helpful if it loses its speed when you put any load on it. The slower turbine might retain its speed under load and thus produce more power. I thought the real goal was generating power?

author: @Guardian_Arias
text:
Curious, I wonder how quickly air could be moved with your cage like fan if you drive it with a motor in a partial ducted fan situation.

It basically is a centrifugal blower fan after all but with some interesting modifications. That should make it better at sucking in air from the outside and blowing it out down the middle. Potentially being extremely good at sucking in a large amount of slow moving air and blowing it out through a relatively small duct either at very high speeds or significant static pressure.

author: @kaboom-zf2bl
text:
tbh the answer is YES .... Force = Mass x Acceleration .... Mass of the object matters .... BUT that object accelerates downward equal to gravity times its mass x the Sine of the angle it is falling at ... (just in case its on a slope)  ....making momentum something to desire as well ... because the faster it goes the more work it can do .... the more mass the more work ... the more speed the more work ... 
.
Mass and Momentum are complimentary parts of the force of the flow of the medium by the generator ... resulting in the force or power generated   Newton Gotta Love him for simplicity ... F=MA ;)

author: @martijnm4472
text:
Hi Robert,
I love your channel! Your exploration of generating energy from many sources inspires me. However as a technician it is hard to grab the power output of your experiments. You are always measuring voltage and that is not indicating power. Would it be an idea that you measure voltage over a dummyload with a certain resistance and measure current, than it is easy (P=I2 x R) to see the power in Watts. Or measure both current and voltage over that resistor so P=UxI can be used. Now you always show the open voltage and the generator does not really get a load attached to it. Generators do slow down if connected to a load. I think you get the point and I gave some extra info for the audience.

Keep going! ❤ from Holland
Martijn

author: @jamest.5001
text: I was just thinking, 😆,     if two generators are built equal, one has 2x the copper of the other, but only spun half as fast, are they equal on the output? Or even close?

replies.author: @arkatub
replies.text: If the extra copper means the windings have double the length then it will double the voltage, if the wire is just twice as thick, with the same number of turns, it will make twice the amps.

replies.author: @kreynolds1123
replies.text:
Questions to better answer.  Serries or parallel.  What is the resistance of the coil, and that is the resistance of the load 

Did you want the second generator to have a second coil in serries or parallel? 

Reguarding double the mass in serries, your doubeling the length of wire but did youbwant to double the length of coil resulting in double the number of turns or did you want to keep keep the length of coil the same but double crosssection of the coil.   They will have close but not the  same output voltage as each other, and  to the the original coil. And either serries wire configuration will have double the resistance across the entire coil so that when you have a load draw a current  the voltage drop across the coil will also double for a given current. 

Reguarding in parallel,  you may wind two coils in parallel and either double the cross section or double the length.  Your combined coil's internal resistance is 1/2 but your output voltage will be close but not identical to 1/2 of the original.

Complicating factors are what is the load resistance that effects current and internal voltage drop and magnetic properties and geometries that effect magnetic fringing under load.  


 or keep winding with the same length but increase the diameter will the cross section of coil is doubled?  Double the length of the coil has more turns than double the cross section so unloaded it would output more voltage than a coil with the same length wire

replies.author: @kreynolds1123
replies.text:
​​​@arkatub  the original problem was if the second generator was spun at 1/2 the speed of the first. 

But even with the additional copper in serries, coil geomitry effect the number of turns. Is the second generator coil twice as long or twice the cross section.  A coil with twice the cross section is not double the number of turns.
